skill.name: uv-package-manager
skill.description: Master the uv package manager for fast Python dependency management, virtual environments, and modern Python project workflows. Use when setting up Python projects, managing dependencies, or optimizing Python development workflows.
skill.summary: A comprehensive guide to uv, the ultra-fast Python package manager written in Rust. Learn installation, project setup, dependency management, and advanced workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation and guide for the legitimate uv Python package manager. The static analysis flagged 103 potential issues, but upon evaluation, all findings are false positives. The detected patterns (external commands, network URLs, filesystem access) are bash code examples within markdown documentation blocks, not executable code. The skill provides educational content about uv installation and usage - a trusted tool from Astral. No malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: Pipe to Shell Pattern
high_findings.description: Installation command uses pipe to shell pattern: `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`. This is the official, recommended installation method from Astral (the creators of uv). While this pattern is flagged by static analyzers, it is a known and accepted pattern for installing development tools from trusted sources.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pipe to shell is the official uv installer from astral.sh - a trusted source. This is a standard installation pattern used by many tools including curl, brew, and npm.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 77 instances of shell command patterns (backticks) in documentation. These are example commands in markdown code blocks for educational purposes, not executable code. Examples include `uv add`, `uv sync`, `uv venv` - all legitimate uv commands shown as documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All detected patterns are within markdown code blocks (```bash ... ```) showing example commands. No actual code execution or user input handling present.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
medium_findings.description: URLs detected in documentation point to legitimate uv resources: astral.sh (official site), github.com/astral-sh/uv (official repo), docs.astral.sh (documentation). These are appropriate references for a documentation skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are legitimate references to uv's official documentation and repository. No suspicious network behavior.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem References in Documentation
medium_findings.description: References to filesystem paths like .venv, .gitignore, .python-version, and cache directories (~/.cache/uv) are standard Python project files managed by uv. These are documented for educational purposes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are standard files and directories that uv creates/manages. Documenting them is appropriate for a uv guide.

content.value_statement: uv is an extremely fast Python package manager written in Rust. This skill helps you set up projects, manage dependencies, and optimize Python workflows with uv's blazing-fast performance.
actual_capabilities: Initialize new Python projects with uv init command
Add, remove, and manage Python dependencies efficiently
Create and manage virtual environments with uv venv
Install and manage multiple Python versions
Generate and use lockfiles for reproducible builds
Integrate uv into CI/CD pipelines and Docker workflows
limitations: Only covers uv package manager, not other Python tools
Does not include runtime code execution or project scaffolding templates
Requires uv to be installed on the system first
Focused on command-line usage, not GUI or IDE integrations

use_cases.title: Quick Python Project Setup
use_cases.description: Rapidly initialize a new Python project with virtual environment, install dependencies, and start coding in seconds instead of minutes.
use_cases.target_user: Python developers who want faster project initialization

use_cases.title: Dependency Management at Scale
use_cases.description: Manage complex dependency trees, resolve conflicts, and maintain reproducible builds with lockfiles for production projects.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and team leads managing Python projects

use_cases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Optimization
use_cases.description: Speed up CI/CD pipelines by using uv for faster dependency installation and cached builds.

prompt_templates.title: Install uv and Create Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me install uv package manager and create a new Python project with a virtual environment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a new Python development environment from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Add Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add dependencies to my Python project using uv. I want to add requests, pandas, and pytest.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding packages to an existing or new Python project

prompt_templates.title: Setup Lockfile
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to use uv lockfiles for reproducible builds and how to integrate them in CI/CD.
prompt_templates.scenario: Explain how to use uv lockfiles for reproducible builds and how to integrate them in CI/CD.

prompt_templates.title: Docker Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to optimize a Python Docker image using uv for faster builds and smaller images.
prompt_templates.scenario: Show me how to optimize a Python Docker image using uv for faster builds and smaller images.

output_examples.input: Initialize a new Python project with uv
output_examples.output:
Run: uv init my-project
This creates: pyproject.toml, .python-version, README.md, .gitignore
Then: cd my-project
Then: uv add requests pandas

Use: uv sync --frozen for exact reproducible installs
Enable caching with: enable-cache: true in GitHub Actions
Install Python: uv python install 3.12

best_practices: Always use lockfiles (uv.lock) for reproducible builds in production
Pin Python versions with .python-version file for consistency
Use 'uv run' instead of manually activating virtual environments
anti_patterns: Do not skip lockfiles in production - they ensure deterministic builds
Avoid installing uv via pip in Docker builds - use the official installer
Do not ignore Python version compatibility - pin versions explicitly

faq.question: What is uv and why is it faster than pip?
faq.answer: uv is written in Rust and uses parallel dependency resolution. It caches packages globally and installs in parallel, making it 10-100x faster than pip.

faq.question: Do I need Python installed to use uv?
faq.answer: No, uv can install itself and Python versions without requiring Python pre-installed. This makes it ideal for new project setups.

faq.question: How do I migrate from pip to uv?
faq.answer: Run 'uv add -r requirements.txt' to convert existing requirements, or simply use 'uv sync' if you have a pyproject.toml. uv is compatible with pip workflows.

faq.question: What are lockfiles and why should I use them?
faq.answer: Lockfiles (uv.lock) record exact dependency versions installed. This ensures every environment uses identical package versions, preventing 'works on my machine' issues.

faq.question: Can uv work with Docker?
faq.answer: Yes, uv is excellent for Docker. Use multi-stage builds with 'COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest' for fast, cached dependency installation.

faq.question: Is uv safe for production use?
faq.answer: Yes, uv is actively maintained by Astral (the creators of Ruff) and is used in production by many projects. It supports signed packages and hash verification.
